message:
feat(fs): add write_atomic for durable file replacement via temp+rename

Implement atomic file write using the standard temp-file-then-rename
pattern for crash-safe file updates:

- write_atomic(): async wrapper that delegates to spawn_blocking_io
- write_atomic_blocking(): creates a temp file in the target directory
  with a unique name (.asupersync-tmp-{pid}-{counter}), writes contents,
  sync_all()s the file, renames into place, and on Unix also sync_all()s
  the parent directory for directory-entry durability
- TempPathGuard: RAII guard that cleans up the temp file if the operation
  fails before the rename succeeds
- unique_tmp_path(): generates collision-free temp names using an AtomicU64
  counter combined with process ID

Exported as public API from fs module alongside existing write().
Includes tests for create-and-replace without temp file leaks and
clean failure when the parent directory is missing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
